reason: world::build_etic_substrate reads recurrence_min as a plain usize threshold over that world's own seeded race set (BTreeMap<RaceId, ValueProfile>), a genuine world-configuration control value with no formula to derive it from and no universal answer.

GaussApprox is explicitly documented and coded as a stamped world-identity method/precision choice folded into the state hash (crates/core/src/gauss.rs), a control value the world carries, not a physical constant and not a composite of anything else.
additive_mutation_step is a field of the per-race GeneticScheme struct in genome.rs, a genuine free per-race parameter (the per-locus additive-step std) with no other value it could be computed from.

environment_variance is a documented per-race field on Race in race.rs, explicitly authored as race data (Principle 11) with the interim zero honest until a future spatial env-field derivation exists; world data is one of the two legitimate homes Prime Directive 6 allows, so this is correctly placed today.

which and how many genes feed each phenotype Channel is defined by a race's own GeneSet (Race.genes in race.rs/genome.rs), an architecture count with no governing formula, so it is per-race data even though it is not yet materialized as one distinct named field.

the design text places the structural rate beside the already-implemented per-race GeneticScheme.mutation_rate (point-mutation) field as its sibling scheme datum; not yet built in code, but nothing suggests it is computable from other values rather than being its own per-race rate.

the curve supplies a soft genetic-distance-to-fertility relationship supplementing the discrete IncompatibilityTable::hybrid_outcome in genome.rs; it introduces a relationship not otherwise modeled in code, so it is a genuine world-dialed response curve rather than a derivable composite.

RingCapacityLaw::capacity_for (axiom.rs) reads one shared Curve keyed only on the being's own expressed Mind.memory value with no race branch, confirmed in code and comments ('never a race identifier'), so it is correctly a single world-level response-function dial rather than per-race data, since per-race variation already emerges through differing memory phenotypes.

the code and basis both state this ceiling is 'independent of any race's memory,' a performance/state-hash/replay budget for that world's build; it fits the per-world 'world's own configuration' bucket rather than fundamental, derivable, or a misplaced per-race value.

entrenchment_threshold(curve, rank) in axiom.rs applies one Curve uniformly to any axiom's integer AGM rank with no race-specific branching, the same world-level response-curve pattern as evidence_ring_curve, so it is a legitimate world dial.

the entry's own STEER FLAG (worksheet 13a) states an absolute per-tick rate bakes in a human-generation lifespan assumption applied uniformly across races; the honest form is a dimensionless lifespans-to-harden dial divided by each race's own lifespan_years field (already present on Race in race.rs), i.e. derived rather than stored as a standalone absolute rate.

a data map of per-interaction convergence-strength magnitudes for the not-yet-built opinion kernel, the same kind of world-level kernel-weight set as the entrenchment and ring curves; no derivation path is available for these magnitudes today.

unit is 'rule' (a function choice, not a decimal); the owner ruling explicitly authors equal-weighting now and defers the member-variance derivation to a later, separately flagged social-depth item, and there is no live non-test consumer yet, so as currently scoped it is a world-mechanism choice rather than a computable composite today.

EpistemicStance::effective_stubbornness (axiom.rs) combines each being's own per-mind dogmatism and freezing fields with one global [0,1] weight; the weight is correctly world-level since per-being/per-race variation already comes through the temperament values it mixes. Note the entry's own audit catch that world.rs currently reads a separate World::stubbornness_split field rather than calling stubbornness_dogmatism_weight() is a wiring-reconciliation bug, not a category/placement defect under this test.

InferenceParams::from_manifest (crates/bio/src/evidence.rs) reads this as one fail-loud engine-wide scalar consumed identically by every mind's commit() test; it is a genuine world-level policy knob (the false-conclusion-versus-cold-case balance for the whole evidence engine) grounded in a cited statistical convention (Wald 1945), not derivable from physics and not itself a per-race datum in the code.

Read alongside commit_threshold in InferenceParams::from_manifest and used uniformly in InferenceFrame::commit's lead-over-runner-up test; a world-level statistical policy constant (inference-to-the-best-explanation margin) with no per-race hook in the code, so it is a legitimate authored world set-point rather than a derivable composite.

InferenceParams.clamp bounds every total at read (clamped_total, commit) and is also passed into good_weight as the saturation bound for a zero-probability likelihood ratio; it is one global certainty ceiling for the whole engine, an epistemic-policy set-point (Thrun/Burgard/Fox overconfidence guard) rather than a physical constant or a computable composite.

ABSENCE_PROBE_MAX (crates/sim/src/absence.rs) is a scan bound that, for a race whose hazard curve never crosses LIFESPAN_HAZARD_THRESHOLD, becomes the presumed-dead ceiling; the code and comments confirm no closed-form derivation exists for a never-crossing hazard, so a world-set bound (large enough for whatever races the world carries, raisable per world) is the honest home, though the value is currently a hardcoded Rust const rather than actually read through the calibration manifest, which is a wiring gap worth flagging separately from the classification itself.

TraceKindDef (crates/sim/src/trace.rs) already establishes the sibling pattern that a trace kind's evidentiary parameters (reliability, false_attribution, decay) are per-trace-kind DATA fields, not manifest scalars, and design.md's Part 9 blockquote lists 'the concealment perceptibility multipliers' in the plural alongside those per-kind curves; concealment itself is unimplemented in code (no concealment field exists on TraceKindDef or the world Trace struct), so authoring it as one global 'evidence.concealment_suppression' ratio would repeat the same authored-global-standing-in-for-per-kind-data pattern the design's own trace-kind registry was built to avoid.

Confirmed in crates/bio/src/evidence.rs: derive_aggregate_diffusion_rate and aggregate_diffusion_rate compute this as sigmoid(gossip.told_weight * gossip.trust_baseline) * contact_density from two already-reserved gossip parameters, with a non-circular test validating it against the real threshold-gated individual gossip loop in world.rs/agent.rs; no independent scalar is read for this key, exactly the 'interim standalone key duplicating an existing curve' pattern the derivable category names.

Confirmed in crates/sim/src/personality.rs: plasticity_at reads TraitDef.plasticity_curve evaluated at Race::maturation_fraction(age), a per-race TraitDef data field consumed by one fixed kernel; there is no manifest scalar named being.plasticity_by_age left to set, matching the derivable category's 'duplicates an existing per-race curve' case exactly.

Confirmed in crates/sim/src/personality.rs: TraitDef.maturity_target is a per-race, per-trait data field the age_personality kernel drifts each trait's setpoint toward (gap = def.maturity_target - cur); the reserved key is already fully replaced by this per-race data field and a fixed kernel, so nothing remains to author as a separate scalar.

No implementation exists yet (no life_event or self_change code found in personality.rs/agent.rs/decision.rs), but the design text lists this magnitude in the same reserved set as maturity-target directions and magnitudes and the plasticity curve, both of which were resolved into per-race TraitDef data fields on the PersonalityRegistry; design.md also frames related mechanism strength as varying 'per race', so authoring life-event-impulse and self-change-burst sizes as a single global manifest scalar would repeat exactly the pre-resolution shape entries 7 and 8 were corrected out of.

crates/foundation/src/value.rs's ValueStructure::Relationship carries a k*k matrix as per-race DATA already (Principle 11), and design.md's Part 21 blockquote separately reserves 'the default compatibility and opposition weights' as the numeric content that seeds/typifies that matrix; this is a legitimate default per-race-structure dataset (a starting content value, not a physics composite), consistent with the value-structure-is-per-race-data design already in code.

crates/foundation/src/value.rs's conflict_pressure takes conflict_coefficient as a caller-supplied scalar multiplying value_dist inside a fixed formula (value_dist * coefficient * (1+niche_overlap) + grievance - trust); the code comment states explicitly 'so the distance-to-pressure mapping is calibrated, not asserted here', a genuine single world-level calibration constant for a fixed kernel, not a computable composite of other reserved/floor values and not itself keyed on race or substance data in the function signature.

The entry's own basis (2026-07-04 re-reservation) states the honest home is a per-race belief_plasticity datum a ValueProfile-level enculturation kernel would read, that Race currently carries no such field, and that no consumer of this key exists yet; grep of crates/foundation/src/value.rs and race.rs confirms no belief_plasticity field or enculturation-pull consumer is present, so the value as currently reserved is a placeholder for what should become per-race data rather than a legitimate standalone global scalar.

Confirmed in crates/foundation/src/value.rs: cross_race_distance folds the measured per-race-pair projection loss from project_to_etic_with_loss directly into the sum-of-squares, and the module doc and a test comment ('the flat incommensurability floor is retired') confirm the flat-floor parameter was removed entirely from cross_race_distance; no constant is read or needed.

Confirmed in crates/sim/src/world.rs's build_etic_substrate: the substrate's axis membership is computed bottom-up by counting, per emic ValueAxisId, how many races carry a stance on it and minting an etic axis only when the count reaches the reserved recurrence_min, in canonical ascending order; membership is a pure function of per-race value-profile data plus the one recurrence_min threshold, so the 'axes' entry itself is a derived set rather than an authored membership list (the remaining reserved input is the single recurrence_min scalar, a different entry).

A per-prime, per-race-adjustable rank grounded in the language-emergence record (33.1, 33.9, record 62.24); it is an empirical typological datum keyed to each prime and race, not a physics composite or a universal constant.

Code (crates/sim/src/typology.rs TypologyParams::from_manifest) reads this as the single free softmax scale via require_fixed, validated against the human WALS/Dryer proportions rather than computed from any other in-engine quantity, with race handled separately through memory_capacity data, so it is a genuine world-level calibration set-point.

Confirmed in code as a manifest-read Fixed (TypologyParams::from_manifest, require_fixed("lang.typology_disharmony")) used directly as a draw-gate probability, and the basis itself states it is a measured cross-linguistic datum grounded in WALS 95A, not derivable from physics.

Confirmed in code: typology::information_weights computes each parameter's weight as the order-2 diversity (Hill number) of its own prior counts, with no manifest key read anywhere in the crate, so the former authored per-parameter table is now correctly a pure function of existing prior data.

SalienceDecayLaw::rate_for (crates/sim/src/language.rs) reads a decreasing owner-authored Curve at the representative memory scalar; the curve shape itself (how fast salience leaks per unit memory) is an empirical/design choice grounded in a usage-recency half-life, not computable from other in-engine values, so it is legitimate per-race/per-world data.

The basis gives an explicit formula, decay_bits >= ceil(2^32 / usage_max_bits), a pure fixed-point representability bound of the kind typology.rs implements as plain Rust consts (WEIGHT_CAP, TILT_MAX) rather than reserved calibration, but no such derivation function exists anywhere in the crate (grep for usage_max_bits/decay_bits finds only the doc comment), so it currently sits as a standalone authored Fixed field (SalienceDecayLaw.floor) instead of being computed from the usage scale's resolution once fixed.

RetentionLaw::window_ticks_for (crates/bio/src/agent.rs) uses this as a base tick count scaled by a separate memory curve; it is a raw simulation-timing choice ("set in ticks against the drift/usage cadence") with no formula tying it to another already-fixed engine value, so it remains a legitimate world-level tunable rather than a derivable composite.

An increasing owner-authored Curve read by RetentionLaw::window_ticks_for at the mind's memory capacity; the curve shape (how much extra retention a sharper memory buys) is empirical per-race data distinct from the salience-decay curve, not a computable composite of other axes.

Confirmed in code: semantics::substrate_quantization computes the step as QUANTIZATION_DIVISOR (a fixed engine constant, 2) of the already-derived concept_thresholds discrimination threshold, with no manifest key read, matching the basis's own "no reserved input remains" statement.

Confirmed in code: value::incommensurability_ceiling is a pure alias over project_to_etic_with_loss's measured per-race-pair projection loss (crates/foundation/src/value.rs), and langdist::lexical_distance/phonological_distance read that normalized [0,1] loss directly, with no standalone manifest scalar anywhere.

The membership of production modalities, reception senses, feature dimensions, and media (design 33.3) is a world-level data registry (siblings of the value substrate and semantic substrate); the design's own reserved-blockquote language calls this "data per world" and no code derives registry membership from anything else.

Confirmed in code: langmod::perceptual_geometry computes formant vectors and pairwise distances through the tube-resonance, sound-speed, and thermoviscous-absorption laws plus per-race sensorium resolution, with the basis stating the entry itself carries no remaining reserved input (the physics axes it reads are separate reserved entries, not this one).

Confirmed in code: langmod::capability_gate/capability_halves computes a channel's capability as the Liebig minimum of body::function_integrity and sensorium acuity, with the one remaining reserved input being a different key (body.function_loss_threshold) that lives on the body module, not an authored magnitude of this entry itself.

Confirmed in code: langmod::acquisition_split is perceive-capability minus produce-capability, both already produced by capability_halves, with the basis and code agreeing no authored rate ratio or reserved input remains.

semantics::concept_thresholds (semantics.rs) is a pure function of the substrate's own min_positive_separation/meaning_span, the perceptual JND, and the contrast budget, with no stored authored threshold; the code confirms the basis's own DERIVES claim, so nothing should be stored under this id beyond the still-open concept drift rate.

langmod::phoneme_priors computes each value's prior purely from the race's own perceptual_geometry confusability plus a capability gate, with UPSID/PHOIBLE demoted to the human race's own resonator-length data row, so no standalone prior table remains to store.

a shared require_map of (candidate sound, base resonator length) read into FeatureValueDef.resonator_length in language.rs is an extensible, world-content registry keyed to each candidate sound's own acoustic identity, not one of the six fundamental constants and not currently computed by the engine from a deeper formula, so it is legitimate registry data the owner supplies.

Race.articulation.vocal_tract_scale (race.rs) is a field the engine already keys per-race, read with no race branch by langmod::articulated_geometry, and anatomy.rs has no resonating-cavity-size datum yet to derive it from, so it is correctly a per-race datum now even though the basis itself flags it for a future anatomy-driven derivation.

Race.articulation.hearing_resolution is a per-race field distinct in code (sensorium.rs) from the [0,ONE] acuity gate, feeding langmod::articulated_geometry's contrast budget as the race's own auditory JND, a legitimate per-race sensory phenotype the engine models no deeper today.

worldbuild::LanguageGenesis.producibility_threshold is a single reserved cutoff applied uniformly in producible_values (langmod.rs) across all races' already-normalized dispersion priors; it is not computed from any deeper composite and not one of the six constants, so it lands in the world/mechanism-data bucket as a generator-floor calibration, though it is not itself keyed to any one entity's data the way a true per-race value would be.

the low end of the coining length range passed to form_system_from_values (langmod.rs) is a flat generator-structural bound with no code path deriving it from inventory size or concept count yet, so it is currently a legitimate authored floor even though the basis itself flags the eventual contrast-budget derivation.

the high end of the same coining length range in form_system_from_values (langmod.rs) is the same kind of flat generator-structural bound as word_min_len, not yet computed from inventory size, so it is a legitimate current placeholder rather than a present-tense defect.

a require_map of per-generation rates for lexical replacement, grammaticalisation, splitting, and borrowing is precisely the extensible, data-defined registry pattern (Part 21/33/36 style) the project treats as legitimate world data, distinct from the dedicated transmission.drift_rate dial.

langdist::distance_component_weights computes each component's weight purely from the pooled corpus's own order-2 diversity (inverse-Simpson Hill numbers) with deterministic residual normalization, and the basis itself states no reserved input remains, confirmed by the code containing no stored weight constant.

language::L2AcquisitionLaw::increment_for reads a single global decreasing Curve at each learner's own race-derived maturation_fraction, so the age-of-acquisition breakpoint already derives from race data, but the curve's shape itself is authored world/mechanism data with no formula computing it, matching the reserved-curve pattern used elsewhere (e.g. SalienceDecayLaw).

no writing/script/literacy mechanism exists yet anywhere in crates/sim/src, so grounding rests only on docs/design.md, which frames this as a bundle of a pressure threshold, script-type continuum weights, a literacy spread rate, and decay/provenance modifiers; these read as legitimate reserved world-mechanism data of the same shape as the rest of Part 33's rates and thresholds, though the continuum-weights component in particular parallels the now-derived lang.distance_component_weights and may turn out to derive from a language's own phonological entropy once built rather than stay authored.

the design doc (33.9, record 62.24) is explicit that this is a determinism safety bound only, deliberately never tied to cohort size or any per-culture data (a rejected steer), so it is correctly an authored engine-wide structural constant rather than a per-race/per-world derived quantity, matching the CLAUDE.md's own example of a performance bound reserved value; no dawn-coordination code exists yet in crates/sim/src to check further.

per the design doc (33.9, record 62.24) each NSM prime carries its own reserved closing threshold read against simulated state (community size, generational depth, contact), a per-prime datum in the same extensible registry pattern as the prime membership and founding-resilience ranks; no code implements the dawn/closing mechanism yet in crates/sim/src, so this is grounded on the design text alone.

eval.rs already derives the curve's x-span (adaptable_range) from the interface axis's own physics range width; only the penalty-depth y-values are reserved, and those are a data-defined per-interface-axis curve (loss physics of that axis), a legitimate per-world/per-axis calibration, not a composite of anything else already present.

ProxyWeights is an open per-proxy-id registry (proxy.rs) whose criticality weight is the physics-units-and-criticality datum of each aggregate proxy in a world's own admitted proxy set; it fails loud if unset (Memo::new), consistent with a legitimate per-world control value rather than a derivable composite.

ProxyRefs.control_efficiency_floor is a standalone SNR-margin-style threshold subtracted from the aggregated chain_efficiency in ProxyKernel::ControlLoopStability (proxy.rs); it is not computed from any join efficiency, interface axis, or other existing datum in the substrate, so it is a genuine new per-world engineering threshold rather than a derivable composite, though its magnitude does not vary per-race in the current type (a single Fixed per Memo/world).

ProxyRefs.resonance_floor is the minimum omega=sqrt(k/m) subtracted in ProxyKernel::Resonance (proxy.rs); no service-load excitation-frequency axis exists elsewhere in crates/physics for it to derive from, so it is a standalone per-world design-load threshold, correctly a reserved scalar rather than a fabricated default (the sqrt(k)/sqrt(m) unit-incoherence bug it replaced is already fixed).

transmission.rs makes drift_rate the irreducible base scale that copy_drift() scales by per-copier fidelity (memory*perception); it is explicitly the base a per-copier value derives FROM, not itself a composite of other reserved values, and Weber's JND is only a calibration anchor, not a formula it is computed from.

The module's own audit note explains the prior pin equal to evidence.decay_rate was invalidated and the value was re-derived from its own erosion-half-life basis; stability_span() derives FROM loss_rate (not the reverse), so loss_rate itself is correctly the independent reserved base, not a composite that should be computed from something else.

TransmissionParams.loss_practitioner_floor (transmission.rs) is read directly from the manifest and used as-is in erode_and_cull; the basis's claim that it equals a population-viability count 'the pool substrate already tracks' could not be confirmed against any existing per-world/per-race population-floor datum in the codebase, so it stands as its own reserved per-world practitioner count rather than a verified duplicate.

Part 54's ruling makes canonical fidelity a deterministic function of in-world significance and the seed; the thresholds gating promotion/demotion are not derivable from physics and are not yet wired to code (no crates/sim source reads this key), but by analogy to the already-set tier.promote_threshold in calibration.rs this is a legitimate world-level scheduling control value, not a composite of other data.

institution.rs's own doc comment on emit_undertaking calls the tier.decision_propensity threshold 'the same reserved pair the crystallization gate reads through emit_undertaking' as inst.crystallization_threshold/inst.crystallization_rate, meaning the aggregate-tier gate is documented as reusing the detailed-tier pair; authoring it as a second, independently named reserved id duplicates that pair rather than deriving from (or literally reusing) it, though the design.md basis's 'per settlement scale' phrasing leaves some room for a genuine scaling factor.

belief.rs's BeliefParams.level_to_strength is a Curve read per race (divergence_comes_only_from_per_race_curve_data test proves two races' distinct curves are the sanctioned source of divergent promoted strengths); it is not computed from any other reserved value, so despite the identity curve being the exact-reconstruction special case, the general monotone curve is legitimately owner/per-race data.

BeliefParams.dispersion (belief.rs) is a standalone half-width magnitude used in instantiate_strength's symmetric mean-zero draw; it is not computed from level_to_strength or any other reserved value (only calibrated against the same reconstruction target), so it is a legitimate independent per-world tunable rather than a derivable composite.

lod.rs's partition_lowest_id already implements the settled lowest-id policy as fixed Rust, not a numeric magnitude; the design text frames it as a one-line data choice only if the owner wants a different fairness policy later, which fits a legitimate (currently defaulted) per-world policy datum rather than a composite needing derivation.

tom.rs explicitly architects the assertion ladder so witnessed/told/said DERIVE from meta.commit_threshold and margin via margin_steps, while reachable (margin_steps: None) is documented as staying 'an independent reserved lever' read directly from the manifest per channel name; this is a deliberate, already-audited per-channel per-world datum, not a duplicate of the ladder derivation.

tom.rs's module doc groups absence with reachable and denied as off-ladder channels (margin_steps: None) that are independent reserved levers by design, distinct from the ladder channels retired into derived form; the entry's own basis text suggesting it be 'matched to the witnessed weight' is calibration guidance for setting the number, not a coded derivation, and the architecture treats it as legitimately separate data.

Design 33.6/33.9 grounds this as a per-culture, per-being empirical propensity (measured blend-versus-switch rate, addressee/register dependent) tied to bimodal-bilingual measurement; it is explicitly a form-of-mixing knob, never a fidelity multiplier or a physics-derivable composite, so it is a legitimate per-race/per-being datum.

Design 33.9 explicitly rejects letting this vary per race/cohort ('never a thinner-language lever tied to cohort size, a rejected steer'), so it cannot be per_world; it is not physics; and its basis cites 'the existing performance-against-emergence budget' as though a concrete budget value already exists to derive from, but no such quantity is established or wired anywhere in the codebase, so as authored it is a free-standing global magnitude in the path of world content (language founding coverage) rather than a derivation.

The recency-window component is explicitly reused/derived from R-LANG-DET's window, but the population-fraction trigger cutoff itself (over-triggering versus late-triggering a homesign dawn or modality shift) is a fresh, genuinely tunable detection threshold with no other value it is set equal to, matching the project's pattern of legitimately reserved detection-sensitivity thresholds.

crates/sim/src/institution.rs FunctionRegistry implements this as an explicit open per-race/per-world DATA registry ('Membership is owner data and grows with the world'; dev_seed is a labelled fallback, not authoritative), the same sibling pattern the project already treats as a legitimate authored affordance floor for the Part 21 value substrate and Part 33 semantic-prime substrate; the entry's own basis proposes an unverified 'derive via clustering' alternative (worksheet reference) that appears in neither the grounded code nor the design document's own Decided-and-reserved text, which still frames axis membership as owner-authored per-race data.

institution.rs recognize() (line ~905-921) consumes this as a caller-supplied per-feature weight vector expressing which features the owner treats as diagnostic of sameness, a subjective design tradeoff with no formula tying it to other existing values.

institution.rs recognize() consumes this as a caller-supplied acceptance threshold trading off over-labelling a novel institution against falling back to a generic description; a genuine calibration tradeoff, not derivable from other data.

This plural/combined id does not appear anywhere in crates/sim/src/institution.rs; the actual implemented mechanism reads the split, more specific inst.crystallization_threshold and inst.crystallization_rate (via CrystallizationParams), so this entry is an interim standalone key that duplicates the now-canonical granular values and should not be separately stored.

institution.rs CrystallizationParams.threshold, read by crystallize()/emit_undertaking, is described as playtest-calibrated ('the intended institution-formation cadence in playtest, the hardest of these to set'); the module docstring frames tier.decision_propensity as 'matched to the detailed tier rate for comparable conditions', i.e. the aggregate-tier value derives from this detailed-tier one rather than the reverse, so this entry is the primary authored calibration point.

institution.rs CrystallizationParams.rate, the per-observation propensity accumulation rate consumed by crystallize(), is a playtest-calibrated cadence value with no cited derivation source; like its threshold sibling it is the detailed-tier original that tier.decision_propensity is matched to, not a value that itself derives from something else.

institution.rs signature_distance()/institution_distance() (line ~958-993) consume this as a caller-supplied per-feature weight vector for the structural distance metric, deliberately split from the recognition weights so the two passes weigh features apart; a genuine diagnostic-weighting design choice, not a physics composite.

No max_depth or recursion-depth wiring exists anywhere in crates/compose/src (grep found none), so it is currently a free-standing authored magnitude; its own basis names two computable quantities, a per-tick compute budget and the depth at which marginal proxy gain falls below the fixed-point noise floor, that should be formalized and derived, the same fix already applied to its Part 41 siblings compose.viability_threshold and compose.transmission_stability.

Confirmed in crates/compose/src/eval.rs and promote.rs: this id literally 'DERIVES to a safety fraction of zero' at the material's own yield/fracture boundary; the free-standing number has already retired in code, with only a separate owner safety factor above zero remaining as a legitimate margin, so the id itself should be computed, not stored.

Confirmed in crates/compose/src/promote.rs: stability_span and drift_similarity_radius are computed set-equal to transmission.loss_rate and transmission.drift_rate (ceil(1/loss_rate), 2*drift_rate), so this id duplicates already-present per-world transmission data rather than being independently authored.

Design Part 41 explicitly calls the integer reuse count a 'cheap surrogate' for the real description-length-decrease criterion, calibrated against an 'aggregate-tier memory budget' that is not established as a concrete quantity anywhere in the codebase; as currently authored it is a free-standing integer standing in for a value that should derive once the description-length machinery exists, the same class of issue already fixed for its Part 41 siblings compose.viability_threshold and compose.transmission_stability.

AccessChannelRegistry/AccessWeights (crates/bio/src/tom.rs) carries no race id; witnessed/told/said were derived onto the assertion ladder from meta commit/margin, but denied and reachable carry margin_steps=None and are explicitly documented as independent reserved levers because no natural ladder offset applies to them, so the value is a legitimate world-configuration constant of the ToM mechanism rather than a misplaced composite or race datum, though no per-race steer scrutiny has yet been run on it as it has for the gossip trust pair.

GossipParams (crates/sim/src/world.rs) is a single global struct with no race field, and crates/bio/src/evidence.rs states explicitly that nothing in the diffusion derivation reads a race id and that two worlds diverge only through their gossip parameters, so told_weight is the legitimate world-level base lever the aggregate diffusion rate is correctly derived from, distinct from the two entries below that carry an explicit per-race steer flag.

The entry's own text is an explicit self-flagged steer note stating the value is a human investment-game figure kept only as a human-analogue interim pending a per-race epistemic-stance axis, and the code (GossipParams in world.rs) currently stores it as one flat global with no race key, matching the defect definition of a per-race outcome authored as a universal global.

Same as trust_baseline: the entry itself states it is a human trust-asymmetry figure that should become per-race data paired with trust_baseline, and GossipParams (world.rs) stores it as a single global with no race keying, so it is currently misplaced rather than a legitimate per-world datum.

LanguageParams (crates/sim/src/language.rs) is read once from the manifest with no per-race or per-culture key, even though Language itself carries a RaceId; design 33.4 frames the drift operators and their rates as reserved procedure-level constants of the naming-game mechanism, with no per-race steer flag raised anywhere in that section, unlike the gossip trust pair.

DriftParams (crates/sim/src/language.rs) is likewise a single global struct read once from the manifest, matching the same pattern and design framing (33.4: "the drift operators and their rates are reserved") as innovation_rate, with no per-race differentiation flagged in the design or wired in code.

The entry's own basis is corroborated by the code: crates/sim/src/census.rs now derives the canonical per-window Ne from the reproductive census (ReproductiveMoments::effective_size composing breeding sex ratio and reproductive-success variance), and the remaining reserved value is confirmed in scenario.rs as a per-world scenario dial (a founder-seed lever different scenarios such as Venus/Europa set to different directions), a legitimate world-configuration set-point rather than a composite or misplaced global.

ReproductionParams (crates/sim/src/world.rs) is a single struct with no per-race field, read once from the manifest and deliberately reused by both the belief-seed inheritance and the mate-preference draw specifically to avoid authoring a second, duplicate lever; this is a legitimate single world-level dispersion scale, not a hidden per-race datum or an unauthored composite.

Confirmed in crates/sim/src/epoch.rs as EpochParams.speciation_dist_threshold, a single per-world field applied uniformly to every pool pair's isolation test during that world's pre-dawn epoch; scenario.rs dials it per scenario (e.g. Crucible dials it Low for fast radiation), the definitional threshold of a specific world's speciation test rather than a per-race trait.

Confirmed in crates/sim/src/epoch.rs as EpochParams.sel_strength, a single scale mapping suitability shortfall to a per-locus coefficient uniformly across every species pool in the pre-dawn epoch of one world; scenario.rs frames it explicitly as a planetary harshness dial (Venus dials it High as "a lethal world selects hard"), a legitimate world set-point rather than the flat mis-scoped global it first appears to be from the entry text alone.

The exact key does not appear anywhere in crates/*.rs or docs/design.md; the mechanism actually built keys mutation per gene (GeneDef.mutation_rate, crates/bio/src/genome.rs ~line 621, fired per locus per reproduction), and design 25.2 defines a per-race GeneticScheme.mutation_default as the fallback for genes that do not override it, so a single flat world-global scalar contradicts the per-gene/per-race-scheme architecture the design and code both establish; the honest home is per-race-scheme (and ultimately per-gene) data.

Design 25.2 places per-interval recombination fractions inside the per-race GeneticScheme.linkage_groups, and the built LinkageGroup.recombination Vec (crates/bio/src/genome.rs ~line 605) is per-gene-interval data whose current code fallback for an unset interval is a hardcoded Fixed::ZERO rather than a manifest read; the entry's own basis ("the default... where a gene does not override it") describes a fallback that the actual per-race-scheme architecture places at the scheme level, not a single flat number expected to suit every exotic genetic scheme alike.

Confirmed in crates/sim/src/epoch.rs as EpochParams.generations, the single loop bound for one world's whole pre-dawn radiation epoch; scenario.rs dials it per world (Europa dials it High, "a vent ecology needs deep time to radiate"), a legitimate world-genesis configuration value.

Confirmed in crates/sim/src/epoch.rs as EpochParams.incompatibility_threshold, a single world-level count applied uniformly to every pool pair's Dobzhansky-Muller isolation test within that world's pre-dawn epoch, matching the same legitimate world-definitional pattern as speciation_distance rather than a per-race or per-gene datum.

It is a field of EpochParams, the per-epoch/per-world configuration struct (crates/sim/src/epoch.rs), a decision threshold for when a joint hybrid cross-probability counts as active that no physics formula pins, so it is a legitimate contingent set-point rather than a fundamental or a computable composite.

Same EpochParams struct (speciation_dist_threshold field) as the allele-presence entry; it is the world's own speciation-rule configuration, an irreducible divergence cutoff rather than a derivable composite.
Same EpochParams struct (incompatibility_threshold field), a small-integer policy decision for the discrete genetic firewall, not physical and not computable from other values.

Read from the CalibrationManifest as the engine's tick-to-world-seconds unit definition (crates/foundation/src/clock.rs base_tick_seconds_fixed); it is a contingent legibility/gameplay choice, not a physical constant, and other reserved values (e.g. time.life_cadence_ticks) are explicitly meant to derive FROM it rather than the reverse.

The entry's own basis text flags it as 'the flagged Earth interim, not a ratified per-world value' with an explicit formula to derive it from world.orbital_period_seconds over time.base_tick_seconds once R-CELESTIAL is wired, meaning a per-world outcome is currently authored as a universal Earth-year global.

race.rs already carries a per-race maturity_years datum (age of reproductive maturity, read by fraction_mature/is_mature) that is the natural basis for a species' own generation length, so authoring one flat 10000-year figure for every race in every world duplicates that existing per-race phenotype as a standalone global rather than deriving the readout from it.

The basis and code (crates/world/src/celestial.rs OrbitalElements::dev_earth) explicitly frame this as owner-set per world, with Earth's value only a labelled dev fixture among many possible worlds, matching the per-world keying test directly.
Same OrbitalElements per-world configuration struct and the same explicit 'owner-set per world rather than an Earth default' framing as orbital_period_seconds.

physiology.rs (derive_base_drain / base_drain_from, line 441 comment 'This replaces the authored base_metabolic_drain') composes the Kleiber basal rate and Stefan-Boltzmann heat loss from a being's own mass, tissue, medium and temperature into the drain fraction; the reserved entry is already a fail-loud DERIVE sentinel rather than a stored magnitude.

physiology.rs derive_exertion_coupling computes the coupling from mechanical work power (laws::power, force*velocity) bridged through metabolic_drain_fraction, so the value is a computed composite of existing body-mechanics data, not an independently authored number.

death_floor is a per-axis field on HomeostaticAxisDef inside the data-defined, extensible HomeostaticRegistry (crates/sim/src/homeostasis.rs), so it keys on each axis's/world's own physiology data exactly as the per_world test requires, and is not computable from anything more basic.

docs/design.md 8.4's own Decided-and-reserved blockquote lists the hidden width alongside other explicitly sanctioned performance-bound reserved values ('set to 4 as a tuneable lever on a compute-tax exploration'); it is a legitimate authored engine/expressiveness set-point, not a physical constant and not computed by any formula in controller.rs.

run_world.rs hardcodes TAXIS_MOVE_BIAS to Fixed::ONE, and the basis itself ties the required magnitude to 'the controller's own activation clamp' (controller.rs activate() clamps to [NEG_ONE, Fixed::ONE]); since any weight at or above the clamp bound saturates identically, the value is pinned by an existing engine constant rather than an independent empirical fact, so it should be read from the clamp bound rather than separately authored.

Same pattern as move_bias: TAXIS_HEADING_GAIN is hardcoded to Fixed::ONE in run_world.rs and the basis explicitly anchors it to the controller's own activation clamp bound, so its magnitude is a fixed-point-scale consequence of an existing constant rather than an independently reserved decimal.

The basis frames the target purely as the magnitude needed to cancel the sibling move_bias weight or saturate the fixed [-1,1] activation clamp (an engine-units relationship to values already present), and the dev harness sets all four taxis gains (move_bias, here_suppress, heading_gain, ingest_drive) to exactly Fixed::ONE, confirming it is a numerics anchor that should derive from the clamp/move_bias rather than being carried as an independent per-world reserved lever.

The basis states the value only needs to saturate the fixed [0,1] INGEST activation clamp decisively, and the dev harness sets it to Fixed::ONE exactly matching that clamp bound, so it is an engine-units anchor to an already-known constant rather than a genuine empirical per-race/per-world datum.

This is the root input EvolveParams::from_layout divides by weight_count to derive mutation_rate; it is not itself a composite of other reserved values, and its basis states a genuine tradeoff (exploration versus intact inheritance) rather than duplicating an existing figure, so it is a legitimate world/epoch-level calibration lever.

The root fraction EvolveParams::from_layout multiplies by ACTIVATION_CLAMP_WIDTH to derive mutation_step; its basis is a real tradeoff (smooth evolution versus exploration) and it is the base input to that derivation, not a duplicate of some other already-present value.

A per-generation evaluation-budget/performance bound on the significance-and-time gradient, the same class of legitimate reserved value CLAUDE.md itself cites as an example of a properly-grounded performance bound rather than a realism figure.

The basis explicitly states it is 'set equal to the pre-dawn radiation depth (biosphere.predawn_generations = 40) for consistency ... rather than an independent figure,' yet evolve.rs reads behavior.selection_generations as its own independent fail-loud manifest key and scenario.rs exercises biosphere.predawn_generations as a separate dial, so the value duplicates an already-present datum instead of being computed from it.

A performance/representability bound (episode length long enough to separate viable from unviable survival against the evaluation budget), not a composite of other reserved values and not universal physics, a legitimate world/epoch-scoped calibration lever.

A per-material property vector sourced from real material datasheets, exactly the per-substance/per-material set-point the category is meant to hold, and the acknowledged floor other mechanisms (wound laws, damage caps) should read from.

crates/compose/src/capability.rs already implements CapabilityCaps::derive, which computes a pressure and a depth ceiling directly from the physics registry's own set axis ranges 'so no ceiling is fabricated,' and BodyParams.capability_depth_max's own comment cites this exact discipline as precedent; yet body.rs's BodyParams.pressure_max/energy_max/stress_max are independently authored reserved scalars (dev value 1000 each) that are merely copied into a CapabilityCaps at line ~750 rather than computed via CapabilityCaps::derive(reg), duplicating a value the floor's own axis maxima already supply.

A universal biomechanical convention (near-total structural loss on a clean fracture) applied uniformly rather than keyed to any other already-present reserved value; plausible as a genuine cross-material modeling constant, though the case for material-specific variance is not disproven.

A genuine shared global anchor: the wound model's own function-loss boundary, which the language capability gate (langmod.rs) is set equal to FOR consistency, i.e. this is the source other mechanisms derive consistency from, not itself a duplicate of some pre-existing value.

The basis frames the value as 'the tissue's thermal-damage threshold (protein denaturation)', a material-specific physical fact, yet body.rs applies params.burn_scale as one global BodyParams scalar (200) uniformly to every tissue and race in the Thermal insult branch, with no per-material keying; this both duplicates the material-specificity already modeled by tissue_properties (fracture strength, elastic modulus, thermal expansion, per material) and fails the alien-feasibility test (a silicon or non-proteinaceous tissue has no protein-denaturation threshold at all), so the honest home is a per-tissue-material datum sibling to tissue_properties rather than a single global magnitude.

The basis itself flags this as needing correction ('a single 0.33 is the mammalian figure applied to every race and fluid. Author per fluid, not as a global default'), and no calibration-manifest key reads body.fluid_critical_fraction anywhere in the crates: it exists only as one hardcoded Rust value inside FluidRegistry::dev_default, so despite living structurally on FluidDef it is not yet actually per-fluid, per-race authored data as Principle 11 requires; the honest home is a real per-fluid entry in the biology registry, sibling to tissue_properties.

clot_rate is correctly homed on FluidDef (per fluid kind), but the paired breach rate is not: body.rs's BodyParams.breach_bleed is a single global scalar (dev value 1/20) applied uniformly at line ~1058 ('params.breach_bleed.mul(rec.severity)') regardless of which fluid the breached pool carries, contradicting the entry's own basis that both are 'the coagulation and haemorrhage timescales of the fluid, per race'; the honest fix is to move breach_bleed into FluidDef alongside clot_rate so it too is per-fluid, per-race data.

BodyParams.torso_mass_fraction/base_thickness (body.rs) is one global struct applied identically to every promoted body regardless of BodyPlan (locomotion count, weapon count, covering), so a real per-race anatomical fact (a quadruped, insectoid, or serpentine race has a very different segment mass split than the human-anchored 0.5 fraction the basis cites) is authored as a single universal instead of keyed on the plan's own data.

strike() in body.rs takes velocity/applied_force/contact_area as plain call parameters (no production caller wires them; only body.rs's own tests supply literals) and the doc comment for the sibling StrikeParams mechanism confirms the project's own direction of retiring authored swing inputs in favor of reading force, cross-section, and stroke off the acting part's own grown body and strength, so these are per-being mechanical outcomes computable from body data, not an authored magnitude.

runner::derive_field_diffusion computes this exactly as k/(rho*c) times dt/dx^2 over the selected medium's own thermal axes, clamped to the stencil stability bound, and FieldCalib::from_resolution (the canonical construction path) never reads the flat field.diffusion key at all, confirming the entry itself documents it as a retained fail-loud fallback sentinel rather than owner content.

It is dx in derive_field_diffusion, the world's own chosen map resolution (metres per tile) that sets spatial scale and Q32.32 representability/stability bounds; it is not a physical constant and not computable from anything else, so it is a legitimate per-world configuration datum.

FieldCalib::from_resolution/from_manifest_with_medium always derives diffusion straight from the scenario's selected medium profile (dense_toxic for Venus, water for Europa) and never reads field.diffusion or its .high sibling, so a single hand-picked ~0.22 standing in for two physically distinct media (a toxic dense atmosphere vs. cold water) duplicates a value the medium-derivation already produces correctly per world.

Unlike diffusion, no derive function exists yet for relaxation (the radiative energy-balance kernels are unwired per the entry's own text), so the lumped Held-Suarez-style forcing-timescale-over-base-tick rate is a legitimate, currently-undeliverable-any-other-way per-world approximation applied uniformly across the field, consistent with a world's own day-night/season period being a global property rather than a per-cell one.

Field::from_map_absolute uses it as the Kelvin offset that reconciles the normalised [0,1] worldgen temperature axis to absolute Kelvin for a specific world (Earth ~288K as the reference), a per-world climate datum with no deeper physical substrate to derive it from.

The paired amplitude term in the same T = mean + range*(normalised - 1/2) reconciliation, a per-world equator-to-pole span (Earth ~60K reference) that is not derivable from the floor and legitimately varies world to world.

Same category as field.relaxation (no derive path exists for the radiative-forcing rate yet); this is simply Europa's own near-static forcing-timescale value, a legitimate per-world instance of the same undeliverable-otherwise datum rather than a composite that should be computed, though FieldCalib::from_resolution hardcoding the plain field.relaxation key regardless of scenario direction looks like a separate wiring gap worth flagging to the owner outside this classification.

physiology::derive_body_exchange_rate computes h*A/(m*c) per being from the medium's convective coefficient, the body's own convective surface, and its own thermal mass, and Runner::set_body_exchange_rate supplies this derived rate on the canonical path; the manifest scalar is retained only as a fixture fallback for a being placed without a body, per the entry's own text.

MetabolicAnchors.kleiber_a is one manifest scalar read once and applied via laws::basal_metabolic_rate to every being in the simulation regardless of race, yet the entry's own basis states plainly that a cold-blooded or exotic metabolism scales the coefficient (though not the exponent), so a per-race quantity is currently authored as a single world-wide global rather than keyed on the race's own physiology/body-plan data.

This is a units-bridge anchor (R-UNITS-PIN) pinning where the normalized [0,1] body_mass trait lands on the kilogram scale for the whole world, set from the world's own largest-taxon size ceiling; the code and design explicitly frame it as not derivable, a legitimate single per-world convention every race's already-normalized trait is read against.

Sigma = 2*pi^5*k_B^4/(15*h^3*c^2) is an exact composite of three of the six closed fundamentals (Boltzmann's constant, Planck's constant, the speed of light), which the classification rubric explicitly rules is not itself fundamental even though the code's own comment calls it a universal physical constant; it should derive from those three rather than being stored as its own reserved decimal.

EmbodiedPhysiology.respiration_transfer_k is one flat manifest scalar read into a single struct field and used uniformly for every being's Fick membrane gas exchange, yet the entry's own basis states it is a per-membrane permeability datum (Fick diffusion coefficient over the membrane's own thickness, set per the membrane's gas permeability against the medium), so a per-race/per-organ material property is authored as one world-wide global instead of keyed on the organ's own material data the way other tissue axes (mat.*, opt.emissivity) already are.

Confirmed in crates/sim/src/medium.rs (MediumField::from_map) and crates/world/src/terrain.rs: it is a per-world sea-level threshold on the world's own normalised elevation field, a control datum keyed to that world's own map, not a universal constant and not computable from anything more basic.

crates/sim/src/physiology.rs explicitly documents that derive_body_exchange_rate (h*A/(m*c), computed from the medium's own convective_coefficient plus the being's own surface and thermal mass, all already-present data) 'replaces the authored field.body_exchange'; authoring a separate Venus-high dial value for this same composite duplicates data already carried by medium.dense_toxic's h and the race's own body composition rather than deriving it.

crates/sim/src/worldbuild.rs reads it as a per-race homeostatic core-temperature set point (manifest.require_fixed), a genuine evolved biological given with no floor-level composite to derive it from; scenario.rs's dial mechanism confirms it is a per-race/per-world control value, matching the per_world definition exactly.

The same per-race set-point axis as the baseline, differentiated for the thermophile race (Venus) via the scenario environment-dial mechanism (crates/foundation/src/scenario.rs), a legitimate per-race biological datum rather than a computable composite.
Same axis, differentiated for the psychrophile race (Europa); confirmed as a surfaced reserved per-race dial value in scenario.rs's own tests (reserved.contains 'physiology.thermal_setpoint.low').

The owner's own resolution (quoted in the entry) explicitly rules the 8K band is authored as a per-race disposition, with generational attunement instead derived from evolvable morphology; this is a direct, grounded per-race datum, not a candidate for further derivation.
Same axis as the baseline half_band (itself confirmed per_world by the owner's ruling), differentiated for the Venus heat-adapted race via the same scenario dial mechanism.
Same axis, differentiated for the Europa cold-stable specialist via the scenario dial mechanism (confirmed in scenario.rs: europa.environment_dial('physiology.thermal_half_band') == Low).

crates/sim/src/medium.rs's MediumSample/require_map profile confirms this is a bundle of per-substance material and biological axes (density, conductivity, specific_heat sourced from Incropera and DeWitt, respirable_content, toxicity, exchange rate) keyed on this medium's own data; the bundled convective_coefficient axis is explicitly documented in physiology.rs as a lumped interim (h is flow-dependent, h=Nu*k/L) whose full Nusselt derivation is deferred because no flow model yet exists to derive it from, so the current per-medium datum is the honest available home rather than a misplaced composite.

Same require_map medium-profile pattern as medium.air, keyed on water's own material data (density, conductivity, specific heat, real-with-source values); the bundled convective_coefficient is the same acknowledged lumped interim pending a flow model, not yet derivable from present data.

Same require_map medium-profile pattern, keyed on the Venus atmosphere's own material data (density, conductivity, specific heat sourced to hot CO2 near 700K); the convective_coefficient axis carries the same documented lumped-interim status as medium.air and medium.water, not yet computable without a flow model.

crates/bio/src/genome.rs documents GeneticScheme::mutation_rate as 'a reserved owner value, supplied as data' per genetic scheme (race); the design document confirms the rate is the owner's free control input that sets the molecular-clock pace under neutral theory (Kimura), not a value derivable from other floor data; the .high variant is the Tempest/Arcanum per-race/per-world dial value of that same control.

Same per-race/per-world mutation-rate control as the .high sibling, the Europa low-drift dial value; confirmed a supplied-as-data owner control per crates/bio/src/genome.rs, not a computable composite.

The per-locus point-mutation probability is documented in crates/bio/src/genome.rs as a reserved owner value supplied as data per genetic scheme; the .high variant is the Tempest/Arcanum dial value of that same per-race control. The noted dependency on genome.loci_per_channel bears on validity bounds (staying sub-threshold), not on derivability, so the value itself remains a legitimate per-race control rather than a computable composite.

genome.rs shows mutation_rate is a field on GeneticScheme, a documented per-race genetic scheme (crates/bio/src/genome.rs:608-621), and scenario.rs's dial mechanism resolves .low/.high manifest keys per named scenario (Europa), so this is a legitimate per-race/per-world set-point on top of an already-per-race field.

additive_mutation_step is a GeneticScheme (per-race) field consumed directly in gamete() to scale the Gaussian additive step (crates/bio/src/genome.rs:622-733), and the .high suffix is a Tempest/Arcanum scenario dial on that same per-race baseline, matching the manifest's dial-per-scenario mechanism confirmed in scenario.rs.
Same GeneticScheme.additive_mutation_step field as the high-end sibling; the .low suffix is Europa's scenario dial on the per-race baseline, consistent with the established dial pattern.

GenePool::select (crates/bio/src/genome.rs:979-993) implements exactly p' = p(1+s)/(1+p*s) as the entry's basis states, s is a per-locus coefficient array (data), and scenario.rs's test confirms venus.dial("genome.selection_scaling") resolves to Direction::High for the lethal-world scenario, so this is a verified per-world dial on a per-locus/per-race selection coefficient.

sound_change_rate is a DriftParams field read once via CalibrationManifest::require_fixed (crates/sim/src/language.rs:551-564), and the generation cadence it multiplies against is itself derived per-race from maturity, so the .high variant is a legitimate scenario-level (Tempest/Arcanum) dial on a properly-derived-cadence mechanism.
Same DriftParams.sound_change_rate field; the .low variant is Europa's placid-world dial, following the same verified manifest-dial mechanism.

calibration.rs confirms lang.drift_operator_rates is a 4-key data map (lexical_replacement, borrowing, plus two others) with matching .high/.low map siblings, the same extensible-registry-plus-dial shape as the other language calibrations; the consuming mechanism itself is not yet wired in language.rs (doc comment marks regular-drift operators as a later Part 33 increment), which affects implementation completeness but not the value's category.
Same reasoning as the high-end sibling: a data map with a scenario dial (Europa, 0.25x), consistent with the pattern, mechanism still pending wiring in language.rs.

innovation_rate is a LanguageParams field read via require_fixed from the manifest and consumed directly in the naming-game coinage roll (crates/sim/src/language.rs:48-63); the .high variant is Tempest/Arcanum's scenario dial on that baseline.
Same LanguageParams.innovation_rate field; the .low variant is Europa's dial, following the same verified mechanism.

AxiomAxisDef.calcify (crates/sim/src/axiom.rs:585-587) is a genuine per-axis (per-race) Fixed field consumed by Axiom::calcify(rate, cap); the .high dial follows the same scenario-differentiated pattern as the other reserved test-world values, though the manifest-to-per-axis wiring is itself still marked deferred in the module doc comment.

entrenchment is an unbounded i32 rank read only through entrenchment_threshold(curve, rank), and Curve::eval (crates/bio/src/decision.rs:76-102) clamps to the curve's own rightmost point beyond its domain, so the practical ceiling on calcification is already implicit in the reserved entrenchment-threshold curve; authoring a separate standalone "cap" (oddly framed as a 0.95 fraction against an integer-rank type, with no per-axis struct field unlike the sibling calcify rate) duplicates information the curve already carries rather than deriving the ceiling from it.

Axiom::weaken(amount, floor) (crates/sim/src/axiom.rs:544-554) takes brittleness/floor as bare parameters with no home on AxiomAxisDef, unlike its sibling calcify rate which is a genuine per-axis struct field; design.md:2144 groups this coefficient among explicitly per-race psychological traits (dogmatism, freezing, stubbornness anchor all live on per-race data), so as currently authored, a single flat global scalar with no per-world dial and no per-race key, it is a contingent per-race trait sitting where per-race data belongs.

calibration.rs confirms axiom.conformity_prestige_strengths is a 2-key map (conformity, prestige) with a matching .high scenario sibling, the same map-plus-dial shape used elsewhere in the manifest; the consuming mechanism itself is explicitly deferred (axiom.rs doc comment: prestige waits on a status system), which is an implementation-completeness note rather than a category error for the reserved dial value.

It is the Dalton wind-coefficient in E=(a+b|u|)*deficit (laws::evaporation_rate, environ.rs:1461-1483), a genuine empirical mass-transfer coefficient that will vary by world once the wind field lands; it is correctly zero now only because the wind input itself is hardcoded to zero, not because the slot is misplaced.

Used only as a min()/overflow-fallback ceiling on evap_a_still*deficit (deficit already bounded by the already-reserved sat_es_cap, environ.rs:1479-1481), and its own basis disclaims it as 'not an owner tuning' and pegs it to Earth's specific pan-evaporation extreme, so with wind currently inert it is a computable composite of evap_a_still and saturation_cap (with headroom) rather than an independent Earth-referenced decimal, and pinning a universal cap to one world's atmospheric extreme risks Terran bias against alien climates.

It is a genuine per-world terrain/timescale set-point (drainage timescale against the world's tick and cell size, subject to a stability bound), read once per manifest and applied uniformly to the downhill-advection law (environ.rs:1254,1350), the textbook per_world control value.

A Liebig denominator of the coarse abstract climate-productivity stand-in (biomass_from, environ.rs:1867-1880), explicitly a per-world calibration of the unmodelled background vegetation rather than a floor axis, so it is a legitimate contingent world datum, not a universal constant.
Same abstract-model Liebig denominator role as water_requirement (biomass_from, environ.rs:1867-1880), a per-world producer-physiology set-point on the latitude-light gradient.
Same abstract-model Liebig denominator role (biomass_from, environ.rs:1867-1880), a per-world growth-temperature scale for the world's producers.

The primary, independently-meaningful Liebig soil denominator (biomass_from, environ.rs:1867-1880), a per-world producer soil-nutrient need; soil_baseline is the derivative of this value, not the reverse.

Its own basis states it 'MUST equal soil_requirement so soil never limits at baseline,' yet the code stores it as a second, independently-settable manifest key (environ.rs:207-208) rather than computing it as soil_req at construction, exactly the duplicate-reserved-scalar pattern the codebase already fixed once for sat_t_ref (environ.rs:189-194).
The basin holding capacity is explicitly set from the volume a cell of the world's own terrain holds at its scale (environ.rs:139-142,1365), a genuine per-world terrain datum, not a universal bound.

The logistic regrowth coefficient is set from the net primary productivity turnover of the world's own producers (stocks.rs Stock, environ.rs:1811,1820), a per-world biological rate that directly shapes the emergent carrying capacity.

The propagule floor is set from the seed-rain density the world's own producers disperse at, small relative to the per-cell capacity ceiling (environ.rs:1811-1819); it does not duplicate any other already-present reserved value, so it stands as its own per-world biological datum.

The salt-mass source term is set from the geochemical weathering rate of the world's own terrain per tick (environ.rs:1538-1539), a per-world geochemistry datum feeding the emergent salt-flat pattern.

The salt-saturation ceiling is set from the salt a cell of the world's own terrain holds at saturation (environ.rs:169,1267), a per-world terrain/solubility datum distinct from and not forced equal to any other reserved slot.

The multiplier converting a world's own salt concentration into the bio.salinity toxin dose the harm law reads (laws::harm_class, environ.rs:170-174,1282) is a world-chemistry conversion factor calibrated against tolerance and Hill-exponent context rather than a duplicate of any already-present value or a universal constant.

Design Part 28 (docs/design.md line 2144) explicitly lists the fission threshold among values that are 'per-race data or reserved global defaults', an owner-sanctioned dual form; the current single flat manifest scalar (crates/bio/src/calibration.rs, read via world.rs::is_fissioning's caller-supplied threshold) matches the sanctioned global-default arm, the same pattern as the sibling axiom.stubbornness_dogmatism_weight, even though heritability/calcify/plasticity were separately promoted to per-race AxiomAxisDef fields.

Same design-sanctioned 'per-race data or reserved global defaults' clause covers the deviation threshold (docs/design.md line 2144, the deviation-flag concept described at line 2142); no is_deviating mechanism or manifest key is wired in code yet (only confidence_weighted_variance/is_fissioning exist for the sibling fission test), so this is an as-yet-unbuilt but legitimately reserved global default, not a misplaced per-race value.

Verified live dial mechanism: crates/foundation/src/scenario.rs's Direction/dial()/dial_manifest_id() resolves scenarios/crucible.toml's declared 'value_metric.conflict_coefficient = high' to this exact manifest id, which value.rs::conflict_pressure reads as a caller-supplied coefficient, a genuine per-world keyed lever.

Same verified dial mechanism; scenarios/europa.toml declares 'value_metric.conflict_coefficient = low', resolved through the identical Direction/dial_manifest_id() path in scenario.rs to this manifest id.

scenarios/tempest.toml and crucible.toml declare 'being.life_event_impulse = high', resolved by the same generic scenario.rs dial mechanism; the owner-ratified stress-test framing in the basis is a policy note on an already-legitimate per-world lever, not a category issue.

Directly exercised in crates/foundation/src/scenario.rs's test (europa.dial('biosphere.predawn_generations') == High) and scenarios/tempest.toml declares the same dial 'high', confirming a genuine per-world keyed value even though the consuming biosphere pre-dawn mechanism itself is not yet built.

Dial confirmed in scenario.rs tests and scenarios/tempest.toml/venus.toml ('genome.effective_population_size = low'); the basis's own note that canonical post-dawn Ne now derives from crates/sim/src/census.rs's effective_size() (Wright-Fisher formulas over real reproductive data) narrows this entry's live role to the pre-dawn founder seed, an initial condition that cannot itself be derived from a census that does not yet exist at world genesis, so it correctly stays an authored per-world set-point, just under a now-narrower scope than its id implies.

Dial confirmed in scenario.rs tests and scenarios/crucible.toml/tempest.toml ('genome.speciation_distance = low'); design.md line 2011 lists the speciation distance threshold explicitly among the reserved per-pool genome calibrations.

Dial confirmed in scenarios/tempest.toml ('genome.speciation_incompatibilities = low') via the same generic scenario.rs Direction/dial_manifest_id() mechanism; design.md line 2011 lists the incompatibility-count threshold among the reserved per-pool calibrations.

The basis ties the slope to 'the world's reference temperature' and 'its temperature-axis scale' (a per-substance Clausius-Clapeyron property that differs by condensing species), but crates/sim/src/environ.rs::EnvironCalib::from_manifest reads a single flat 'hydrology.saturation_slope' key with no dial variant anywhere in scenario.rs or the scenarios/*.toml files (unlike genome.*/value_metric.*/being.* which do have .high/.low dials), so every world from Mirror to Venus to Europa currently shares one slope regardless of its condensing chemistry, contradicting the value's own claimed per-world grounding.

The basis explicitly calls this 'the physical saturation pressure of the world's condensing species at that temperature', an inherently per-substance/per-world datum, but crates/sim/src/environ.rs reads it as a single flat 'hydrology.saturation_e_ref' manifest key with no per-world dial or per-substance keying in code, so a water-condensing world and a wholly different-chemistry world currently receive the identical value.

The basis frames this as a pure engine representability ceiling ('not an owner tuning of the climate...not a floor axis, not a physical value to derive'), the same role true fixed-point overflow guards play elsewhere in the codebase (laws.rs's R_SAT_N2/R_SAT_N3/V2_MAX, which are hardcoded Rust consts explicitly 'Forced by Q32.32, not a reserved realism value', never owner-manifest entries); but here the number is instead placed in the owner-facing calibration manifest and its magnitude is hand-derived from Earth's own temperature range ('Earth hottest ~55C...3x headroom'), a Terran-anchored heuristic that could under-cover a hotter or chemically different world (e.g. scenarios/venus.toml) and silently clip real saturation pressures rather than merely guard overflow; the honest form is a hardcoded constant computed from the Q32.32 representable ceiling, matching its stated engine-mechanics role, not an owner-reserved decimal sized off one world's climate.

The basis frames this as a coarse, deliberately unmodeled lumped efficiency ('the engine models condensation no deeper than this lumped rate (no cloud microphysics)'), matching CLAUDE.md's own example of a legitimate reserved basis (a modelling/performance bound rather than a realism claim), and unlike the saturation_slope/e_ref/evaporation_still entries it does not explicitly claim to be a property of 'the world's' own condensing chemistry, so the lack of a per-world dial is a lesser mismatch; it remains a legitimate authored control knob.

The basis explicitly states 'Set from the world's still-air mass-transfer coefficient', a per-world/per-atmosphere physical quantity (Dalton's coefficient depends on atmospheric density and viscosity, which differ sharply between e.g. Venus's dense CO2 atmosphere and Europa's thin one per their own scenario files), but crates/sim/src/environ.rs reads 'hydrology.evaporation_still' as one flat global manifest key with no per-world dial anywhere in scenario.rs or scenarios/*.toml, so the claimed per-world grounding is unmatched by the actual wiring.

The doc comment and code (physiology.rs) state this global scalar stands in for emissivity times radiating area, and covering_emissivity() already reads the being's own covering material axis (OPT_EMISSIVITY) while area is derivable from BodyPlan, so the honest form is to compute the product from existing per-body data rather than store one global authored lever.

The code's own SEAM comment in affordance_percept.rs flags this as a perceiver-independent embodiment-wide global standing in for the perceiving being's own delivered stress, which should be computed from that being's own force/geometry data rather than authored as one flat MPa scalar for every perceiver.

discovery.rs reads exploration_floor directly from the manifest as its own independent weight-ratio dial with no formula tying it to other stored values, a legitimate world-level exploration/exploitation control knob the owner sets directly.

DiscoveryCalib::from_manifest in discovery.rs literally reads reward.noise_floor for this field (m.require_fixed("reward.noise_floor")), confirming it is already correctly derived by construction rather than an independently authored value, so the reserved entry is documentation of a derivation, not a stored dial.

discovery.rs reads surprise_gain as its own manifest key with no in-code formula tying it to other stored quantities; the basis only recommends calibrating it 'against' other scales for consistency, so it remains a legitimate independent behavior-tuning control value.

planning.rs takes depth_cap as a plain usize cognition-budget parameter with no manifest wiring or derivation yet, explicitly a performance/resolution bound the owner sets from profiling rather than a composite of other stored values.

plan_chain in planning.rs takes hop_cap as an independent usize step-length budget, distinct in role from depth_cap and not computed from any other floor or per-race data in the current code, a legitimate performance-bound control value.

conviction_experience.rs reads retention as its own manifest key (felt_conviction.retention) and the doc explicitly states it is deliberately NOT tied numerically to reward.eligibility_decay, an independent lifetime-integration-window control value the owner sets.

The struct doc in conviction_experience.rs states outright that this flat scalar is a dev interim standing in for axiom.entrenchment_curve read at the axiom's rank, which is already the canonical per-axiom entrenchment gate, so the honest form is to derive from that existing curve rather than store an independent flat threshold.

The struct doc in conviction_experience.rs states this flat scalar is the interim until it reads Mind::plasticity, the per-being phenotype the axiom kernel already uses for the evidential move, so the honest form is to derive from that existing per-being phenotype rather than author a separate flat step scale.

runner.rs reads promotion_stress_threshold directly from the manifest as an independent reserve-fraction gameplay/narrative-promotion dial with no derivation from other stored per-being or physical data.

runner.rs reads promotion_budget directly from the manifest and converts it to a usize count with no in-code formula deriving it from a stored per-tick dialogue cost or frame-budget value, so it stands as an independent engine performance control value.

morphogen.rs reads mat.turnover_rate as a per-segment floor material axis off the being's own grown tissue (seg.mat("mat.turnover_rate")) in accrue_aging, exactly the legitimate per-material/per-race datum shape the per_world category describes.

runner.rs does not read any manifest key for this value; it passes the existing STRESS_GUARD fixed-point overflow constant directly as accrue_aging's energy_max argument, confirming the entry is already correctly reused/derived as a representability cap rather than stored as its own authored world quantity.

It is a world-scale hydrology calibration constant (the dilution water-scale for the salt/(water+reference) ratio), sibling to EnvironCalib's other legitimate per-world environment set-points (salt_cap, salt_weathering, precip_rate), set from the world's own dry-cell soil-water scale with no existing quantity in the code it duplicates.

worldbuild.rs wires World::set_stubbornness_split from the key "belief.enculturation_stubbornness", but axiom.rs already defines the identical dogmatism/freezing split weight effective_stubbornness applies as the separately reserved, separately documented, separately tested "axiom.stubbornness_dogmatism_weight" (its own accessor stubbornness_dogmatism_weight()); the honest form is to read that existing key (the way HarmLearningCalib/RewardLearningCalib both alias evidence.log_odds_clamp for certainty_clamp) rather than author a second duplicate value.

A genuinely distinct mechanism (the aggregate belief-pool SI-logistic diffusion beat, separate from the per-individual enculturation Friedkin-Johnsen beat) with no duplicate key found; a legitimate world-level contagion-speed control value, currently inert/armed only.

runner.rs applies one flat global harm_noise_floor uniformly across every homeostatic axis (emb.homeo.axes.iter().any(|axis| is_harm_tick(delta, harm_learn.harm_noise_floor))), yet HomeostaticAxisDef::base_drain (the quantity the basis says this equals, scaled to the tick) is already a computed per-axis field that differs by axis (1/400 vs 1/300 in the dev fixtures); the honest form is to derive the floor per axis from that axis's own base_drain rather than store one authored scalar for all axes.

feature_bucket applies one flat granularity to every class in a PerceptRegistry, but the registry is explicitly built to carry multiple classes with different natural scales (PerceptRegistry::from_tolerances over several toxin classes), and the basis itself claims the value is "the sensorium's per-class just-noticeable difference"; the honest home is per-percept-class data rather than one number shared by every sensed substance.

observation_weight() is explicitly documented as "General over the two probabilities, reading no kind, race, or feature" (a deliberate Principle-8/9-consistent choice, since the being cannot read hazard identity to key its own reliability on it), and no already-wired dose-response quantity exists in code to derive it from, so it stands as a legitimate authored world-level constant of the interoceptive-harm channel's reliability.
The sign complement of harm.p_harm_given_harms, sharing the same deliberate kind/race/hazard-blind design and the same absence of an existing quantity to derive it from in code.

The exact sign-mirror pattern of harm.noise_floor: runner.rs applies one flat reward_noise_floor uniformly across every homeostatic axis via is_reward_tick, while its own basis ties it to a per-axis resting recovery rate that (like base_drain) is a per-axis physiological quantity; should be derived per axis rather than stored as one authored scalar.

Its own basis states it is set to "the same acuity the harm learner buckets on," yet it is wired through a separate manifest key ("reward.feature_granularity") rather than reading harm.feature_granularity, unlike the codebase's own established pattern of aliasing a shared key (both HarmLearningCalib and RewardLearningCalib read the identical evidence.log_odds_clamp for certainty_clamp instead of authoring a duplicate).

discovery.rs uses the identical percept::feature_bucket function and the basis explicitly states this value "carries the same basis harm.feature_granularity and reward.feature_granularity carry," yet it is authored through a third independent manifest key rather than reading/aliasing the shared granularity, the same duplicate-key defect as reward.feature_granularity.

The mirror of harm.p_harm_given_harms: observation_weight() is documented as general over kind, race, and action, a deliberate design choice with no already-wired per-action quantity in code to derive it from.
The mirror of harm.p_harm_given_benign, sharing the same deliberate generality and absence of a derivable source quantity in code.

A genuine TD-lambda tunable for the action-to-reward interoceptive lag, only BOUNDED by (not equal to) the belief substrate's existing retention window per its own basis, with no code evidence of an already-present quantity it duplicates.

A distinct lag (the being-signal-to-harm cue delay) from reward.eligibility_decay; its basis says it is "set relative to" the reward sibling, a design constraint rather than a literal equality/duplicate, and no code path computes it from an already-present field.
